name: QRadar-0003 - SQL Injection Attempt Detection
description: Detects SQL injection attack patterns in web application logs, WAF events, and IDS/IPS alerts. Looks for known SQLi payloads such as UNION SELECT, OR 1=1, DROP TABLE, and encoded variants commonly used to bypass basic filters.

detection.log_source_type: Web Application Firewall (WAF), Apache/Nginx Access Logs, IDS/IPS
detection.aql: SELECT sourceIP, destinationIP, URL, username, COUNT(*) as injection_attempts FROM events WHERE LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%waf%' OR LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%apache%' OR LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%nginx%' AND (UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%union%select%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%or%1=1%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%drop%table%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%information_schema%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%benchmark%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%sleep(%) ' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%waitfor%delay%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%%27%' OR QIDDESCRIPTION(qid) ILIKE '%sql injection%') GROUP BY sourceIP, destinationIP, URL, username HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3 LAST 10 MINUTES

response.offense_description: SQL injection payload patterns detected in HTTP requests. Source IP is probing for database injection vulnerabilities.

notes: Inspired by SIGMA rule: web_sqli_indicators and OWASP Top 10 A03:2021 - Injection. Tune URL patterns to match your web application structure. Consider correlation with WAF block events.
author: Security Team
